_comment: Labs/Demos card source; index.html renders into .projects-grid. featured = full-width, first in section; visible = regular. themes (controlled vocab: agentic, robotics, vision, learning, extension) powers the filter bar — omits 'in-browser' (the whole site's premise, not a slice); no themes → 'All' only. topics = display-only pills; set ONLY on private/org-owned cards, since public jonasneves/* pull topics from GitHub via enhanceLabsAndDemos(). publicRepo:true = static GitHub link for org-owned public repos absent from the fetched list; skip for private (link 404s; owners get it via the enhancer). webstore = Chrome Web Store URL → store badge, set even if href already points there. icon = sprite-symbol id (no icon- prefix) from index.html. desc: cold-reader copy (what → for whom → credibility hook); visible descs ≤ ~100 chars for the 2-line clamp.

featured.name: OPAL — Orchestrated Parallel Agent Loops
featured.desc: Drive full agentic Claude Code from any browser — direct over WebRTC, no relay, no tunnel. GitHub-gated; runs on your machine, on your Claude subscription.

featured.name: Better Robotics
featured.desc: Browser-native robotics education — pair an ESP32 or Raspberry Pi over Bluetooth and drive it with JavaScript. Duke Pratt research led by Prof. Jon Reifschneider.

featured.name: Pip
featured.desc: Drop-in assistant bubble + panel for any site — ESM, no build, you bring the brains. WebRTC to a live operator or an in-browser model. It's the bubble on this page.

featured.name: Cuko
featured.desc: Predict-then-reveal study — built-in subjects on the web, or quiz any page with the extension.

featured.name: CanvasFlow
featured.desc: AI assistance and schedule planning inside Canvas LMS — on the Chrome Web Store.

visible.name: ros-mcp
visible.desc: Drive ROS 2 robots from an AI agent — topics, services, and actions as MCP tools.

visible.name: Student Benefits Hub
visible.desc: Student-tools directory curated end-to-end by an AI agent.

visible.name: TapTo
visible.desc: Reprogrammable NFC & QR tags — repoint anytime without rewriting. On the Chrome Web Store.
